name: wsl-interop
description: Teaches a WSL2 Claude agent to reuse the Windows host toolchain via interop instead of reinstalling everything on Linux. Agent-facing skill (the human-only cd helper lives in wsl-clipboard-cd).

methods.name: wsl-only
methods.command: grep -qi microsoft /proc/version 2>/dev/null && echo wsl-interop: no software to install, guidance skill only || echo wsl-interop: not WSL, this group is a no-op here

install.notes: No external software. Pure guidance skill. Install INSIDE WSL only (grep microsoft /proc/version). On Windows and native Linux the advice does not apply, so decline it there — the installer never auto-installs a new group on --update/--sync, it only prompts once and records the choice in the known-groups ledger.
